translation.en: They will take care of the green trees.

notes_md:
**chăm sóc** = to take care of, look after someone or something. It’s about giving attention, time, and effort so they stay healthy or in good condition.

Used with people, animals, plants, health, skin, hair, etc. Very common for family care and health-related topics.


You’ll also hear **chăm sóc cho + ai** when focusing on “for someone,” but the direct form is more common.  
Close word: **lo (cho)** = worry about / take care of (less hands-on, more about concern).

example.vi: Tôi luôn chăm sóc cây cối trong vườn.
example.es: Siempre cuido las plantas en el jardín.
example.en: I always take care of the plants in the garden.

definitions.en: To look after someone or something; to provide care.
definitions.es: Cuidar a alguien o algo; proporcionar atención.
definitions.vi: Để chăm sóc ai đó hoặc cái gì đó; cung cấp sự chăm sóc.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I always try to take care of my family well every day.
Can you help me take care of the plants in the garden?
The doctor told me to take care of my health more carefully.
notes:
chăm sóc = to take care of, look after someone or something. It’s about giving attention, time, and effort so they stay healthy or in good condition.

Used with people, animals, plants, health, skin, hair, etc. Very common for family care and health-related topics.

You’ll also hear chăm sóc cho + ai when focusing on “for someone,” but the direct form is more common.  
Close word: lo (cho) = worry about / take care of (less hands-on, more about concern).

translation.en: The doctor will take care of the patient.

exampleSentences: Anh luôn cố gắng chăm sóc gia đình thật tốt mỗi ngày.
Chị có thể giúp em chăm sóc cây cảnh trong vườn không?
Bác sĩ dặn em phải chăm sóc sức khỏe cẩn thận hơn.
